init(IPartyPoolDeployer.DeployParams).details: Validates all DeployParams fields. Length-and-bound checks for `bases` and         `fees` ride alongside the existing checks; everything is in one pass so the         creation-code byte count stays small.

collectProtocolFees(address).notice: Transfer all protocol fees to `dest` and zero the ledger.

enforceTransferLocks(address,address,uin.notice: Mint-lock bookkeeping for the ERC20 `_update` hot path. Lifted         off PartyPool so the cohort-migration code is held inside this         deployed library and doesn't count against PartyPool's         EIP-170 runtime budget. Callers (PartyPool._update) invoke         this BEFORE `super._update` so the lock state is already         consistent when the parent debits `_balances[from]`.         Behavior:         - `from == 0` (mint via OZ ERC20 path): no-op. Pool-internal           mints go through `_erc20Mint` directly and don't reach here.         - `from != 0 && to != 0` (transfer/transferFrom): prune           `from`'s expired cohorts; if the debit dips into the           sender's locked region, migrate the smallest FIFO prefix           of cohorts covering the excess to the recipient.         - `from != 0 && to == 0` (defensive ERC20-burn branch — the           pool's own burn paths use `_erc20Burn`, not this one): keep           the hard `balance − value ≥ locked` revert. A future           ERC20-style burn must not redeem locked LP.

init(IPartyPoolDeployer.DeployParams).notice: Constructor body for PartyPool, factored into this library so the pool's         creation code stays small enough for `PartyPoolInitCode` (which embeds the         creation code as a runtime constant) to satisfy EIP-170. Runs once via         delegatecall during PartyPool's constructor.         Returns the address of the "BFStore" SSTORE2 data contract that holds the         pool's bases and fees; PartyPool captures it into its `IMMUTABLE_BFSTORE`         immutable. Immutables can't be read across delegatecall during construction,         so PartyPool assigns its other immutables (KAPPA, WRAPPER, etc.) inline         before this delegatecall and reads them locally afterwards.

lockedBalanceOf(address).notice: Sum of LP currently locked for `account`. View-only — moved off         PartyPool to keep its deployed bytecode under EIP-170.

mintAmounts(uint256,uint256,uint256[]).notice: Calculate the proportional deposit amounts required for a given LP token amount
